text:
In the first scene, I am struck by the stillness that glass imposes between myself and the group of people. They are engrossed in their own concerns, and I feel my own presence as distanced, an observer rather than a participant. There is a sense of anticipation or curiosity, as if I am waiting to see if the boundaries between observer and observed might dissolve.

In the second scene, the vivid wall and digital displays give me a sense of intellectual excitement. I feel energized by the atmosphere of innovation and research. Here, I am reminded of how environments deliberately invite us to rethink and reshape our ideas. I feel more engaged, alert, and open to new insights, as if surrounded by the pulse of unfolding possibilities.
